mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when the unemployment rate remains above 10%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, which amplifies corporate earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when only one series rises, or the interaction term yields a statistically insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-crisis recovery phase reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal predicts negative returns during late-cycle expansions or fails to generate positive alpha when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth rates, or the signal reverses direction during stagflation periods with rising inflation and stagnant output.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.3% monthly threshold forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive despite inflation spikes, or the signal fails to predict negative returns when real rates remain deeply negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm structural labor market improvement, which sustains consumer demand forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when payroll growth is driven solely by temporary government hiring, or forward returns turn negative when the unemployment rate plateaus above 9%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 550,000 units annually during a credit-constrained environment signal a bottoming real estate cycle, which triggers short-term sector rotation into financials and industrials, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate positive returns when mortgage rates rise above 6%, or housing starts revert below 500,000 within two months of the trigger.
